intro: Ammi visnaga poultice, also known as sweet cicely, is an herbal remedy used to treat various skin conditions, including wounds, eczema, and acne. The poultice is typically made by mixing the dried roots of the ammi visnaga plant with a carrier substance, such as flour or water, to create a paste that can be applied directly to the affected area. When applied as a poultice, ammi visnaga is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and heal the skin.
side_effects: Ammi visnaga poultice can have side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and potential interactions with medications, particularly blood thinners. These side effects are often mild and temporary, but in some cases, they can be severe. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using ammi visnaga poultice, especially if you have a history of allergies or are taking medications.
